normalization_point: Loop 3 lowers battery-material scores because EV CAPA and contracts can quickly become 4C.
Battery equipment is Watch-to-Green only when CAPEX becomes delivery, revenue, and margin.
Recycling/second-life stays Watch-to-Green but now requires SOH and grading-cost validation.
ESS LFP grid storage is split out because value, duration, customer, and GWh can make a cleaner Stage 2/3 path.
EV infrastructure needs utilization and unit economics, not charger-count headlines.
Hydrogen CAPEX can reach Stage 2, but Green needs customers, utilization, and OP conversion.
Solar stays RedTeam-first because subsidy and US manufacturing can break on customs or supply-chain detention.
Wind/PPA policy stories remain Watch until project economics survive rates, permits, and foundation costs.
Waste treatment remains the clearest R3 Green-capable axis when permits, volume, and recurring FCF are proven.
Carbon price is not enough; compliance revenue and cost pass-through are the stronger route.
Data-center water reuse needs named customers and economics before Green.
EV fire/certification/recall/regulation overlay is a RedTeam gate, not positive score.
SOH opacity is a battery recycling and second-life RedTeam gate, not a Green input.
Lithium is a cycle overlay by default; ESS demand can help Stage 1/2 but does not make Green alone.
